name: Spring Xian Bing
ingredients: 0.5 tsp Green sichuan oil 青花椒油
10 g Aged White Soy屏東白醬油
steps: Simmer glass noodles for 5min until cooked but still firm.
Strain the dashi and keep the liquid.
Rinse the glass noodles under cold running water to stop it from cooking further.
Cut the fennel to cube size, oil it gently.
Roast the cubed fennel in josper for about 5min, keep an eye on it.
Use a scissors to cut down the cooked glass noodles to 2cm lengths.
Chop the spring onion to very fine rings.
Slice the wild garlic really thin, and about 3cm long.
Chop the dills to 2cm wide, do not include the end stems.
In a mixing bowl, put in the weighed glass noodles.
Season the glass noodles with Aged white soy, white pepper, kow choi oil and green sichuan oil.
Taste, add a pinch of salt if it needs it.
Roll and pan fry like xian bing and serve with salted plum juice mirin.

notes: Make fresh batch everyday. DO NOT use any leftovers overnight. It's a spring xian bing, it needs to be fresh!!!!
Glass noodles should always be 30% of the total herbs weight.
other_details.serve_with: Salted plum juice and mirin dip
